channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Where Do UN Counterterrorism Funds Go? It’s Murky.
item.description: Counterterrorism at the United Nations has enjoyed a meteoric 700 percent increase in regular budget funding over the last three years. Now, as UN-wide austerity takes hold, and most UN entities need to prove their worth, increased scrutiny is coming for one of the murkiest sectors of the organization’s peace and security architecture. The UN ... Read more

item.title: UNDP-backed disability project becomes flashpoint in Sierra Leone
item.description: An NGO alleges violence and arrests at a project site linked to the U.N. Development Programme.

item.title: Imagery, Algorithms, and the Ballot: What Takaichi’s Victory Says About Youth Politics in the Digital Age
item.description: Sanae Takaichi’s electoral victory in February marks a historic turning point in Japanese politics. As Japan’s first female prime minister and the leader of a commanding parliamentary majority, she represents change in both symbolic and strategic terms. Conventional wisdom long held that younger Japanese voters leaned progressive, were sceptical of assertive security policies, and […]

item.title: Devex Invested: At EIB meetings, big money in a tiny duchy
item.description: The EIB Group Forum comes at a pivotal moment for Europe’s economic strategy, as EIB says it’s “carrying the flag of development.” Plus, from Nairobi dealmaking to the fight over AI infrastructure, development finance is tilting toward strategy and control.

item.title: EIB says it’s ‘carrying the flag of development’
item.description: The European Investment Bank pledges $1 billion for African electrification, expands vaccine financing, and launches its third Gender Action Plan, even as it boosts European defense investments.

item.title: Get ready to push back against the government, US NGOs told
item.description: The U.S. government has upped the pressure on NGOs. Should the sector respond by keeping a low profile and hoping that the administration's attention moves elsewhere, or prepare to push back?

item.title: Devex Newswire: Amid aid cuts, does localization still matter?
item.description: With the current reevaluation of foreign aid, is localization still a priority? Plus, what to know about Anthropic’s deal with Rwanda.

item.title: Financing Africa’s Biodiversity Conservation With Dwindling Donor Support
item.description: Relying on donor funding is not the right way to finance biodiversity conservation. Biodiversity is not a charitable cause. It is actually part of the sovereign natural assets, and so we need to look at ways in which countries can link their economies to biodiversity conservation. - Luther Bois Anukur, IUCN Eastern and Southern Africa

item.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Fourth day of escalating conflict between US, Israel and Iran
item.description: Violent escalation in the Middle East has entered a fourth day, with US and Israeli military strikes against Iran and ensuing Iranian counter-strikes hitting targets across the region, disrupting airspace and transport routes. The situation has caused civilian casualties and raised fears of broader instability, including in Lebanon and the Gulf. We will bring you real-time reporting and updates from across the United Nations system. UN News App users can follow the coverage here.

item.title: Iran crisis: Schoolgirls killed, thousands displaced and aid compromised
item.description: On the fourth day of Israeli and United States airstrikes against Iran and amid growing violence and instability in the Middle East, the UN urgently called for protection of civilians and warned of growing displacement and humanitarian needs.

item.title: How do we Navigate Asia-Pacific’s Climate-Cyber Polycrisis?
item.description: Communities globally are increasingly exposed to overlapping threats. Extreme weather, health emergencies and cyberattacks are occurring more frequently and simultaneously, often interacting in ways that amplify risks and strain response systems. Experts describe this as a polycrisis, where threats converge, creating a complex pressure point for governments, businesses and communities. Today, a polycrisis is brewing […]

item.title: A New World Order Where Might is Right
item.description: As the build-up for a proposed “new world order” continues, a lingering question remains: will the country with the most powerful military reign supreme? The United Nations remains politically impotent. The UN charter is in tatters. The sovereignty of nation states and their territorial integrity have been reduced to political mockery. And the law of […]

item.title: How UNDP & Global Partners are Tackling Root Causes of Violent Extremism in Ghana’s Borderlands
item.description: Across the world’s fragile borderlands where insecurity, climate stress, and marginalization intersect, communities often find themselves on the frontlines of violent extremism. Yet these same communities also hold the greatest potential for peace, when given the confidence, tools, and opportunities to shape their own future. In northern Ghana, through the catalytic support of Denmark, Luxembourg, […]

item.title: At Melania Trump’s UN Briefing on Children, Silence on Iranian School Strikes
item.description: United States First Lady Melania Trump presided over a United Nations Security Council meeting on Monday to talk about the impact of conflict on children, barely two days after schools were hit and children were killed amid the US-Israeli attacks in Iran. The first lady chaired the Council meeting as the US began its rotating ... Read more
